items.body: The product shall provide an ANIP fronting service for Linear that exposes only organization-approved product-work capabilities for issue discovery, issue preparation, comment preparation, status transition requests, and cycle move requests. The service shall not expose raw Linear workspace tools or arbitrary GraphQL operations to agents.
items.rationale: Both sources explicitly frame the product as a governed fronting layer whose contract owns capability semantics and intentionally hides raw tool exposure.
items.title: Expose a governed Linear capability surface instead of raw workspace tools

items.body: The product shall support read-only search of Linear issue context using a required team key and query, with bounded result limits and actor-visible summaries only. Search behavior shall remain constrained to approved team scope and must return clarification_required when required context such as team scope is missing or ambiguous.
items.rationale: The operation starter and source spec both define search as bounded, team-scoped, read-only, and clarification-driven when context is missing.
items.title: Support bounded, team-scoped issue context search

items.body: The product shall prepare issue creation previews and issue comment previews without creating or posting anything directly. Issue preparation shall collect required team, title, and description inputs and may include governed metadata such as project, labels, and other approved optional fields. Comment preparation shall collect issue, purpose, and bounded context and return a preview suitable for review before posting.
items.rationale: The source repeatedly states that issue creation and comments are preview-only flows that stop before mutation.
items.title: Require preview-first behavior for issue and comment creation flows

items.body: The product shall treat issue creation preparation, comment preparation, status transition requests, and cycle move requests as approval-gated flows. For each of these actions, the service shall stop at a preview or request artifact and require explicit approval before any downstream mutation is allowed to proceed.
items.rationale: Approval-gated behavior is central in the source spec and project summary, especially for write-adjacent operations.
items.title: Gate all write-adjacent Linear actions behind approval boundaries

items.body: The product shall support governed status transition requests for a specified issue and target status, with a required business reason. The service shall return clarification_required when issue or target status context is missing or ambiguous, and it shall enforce explicit grant requirements for restricted workflow states rather than allowing direct workflow bypass.
items.rationale: The source defines status changes as approval-gated requests with required reason capture and explicit restrictions for certain workflow states.
items.title: Govern status transition requests with reason capture and restricted-state controls

items.body: The product shall support governed requests to move an issue into a target cycle using a required issue reference, target cycle, and business reason. The service shall stop for approval before any move occurs and shall require explicit grant for cross-team moves.
items.rationale: The source spec explicitly defines cycle move requests as approval-gated and calls out cross-team moves as requiring explicit grant.
items.title: Govern cycle move requests and restrict cross-team moves

items.body: The product shall use explicit clarification semantics when required context is absent or ambiguous. At minimum, missing or unclear team, issue, target status, or target container context shall not be guessed and shall instead produce clarification_required so the caller can supply the missing information.
items.rationale: Clarification behavior is directly specified in the policy semantics section and reinforced by per-input clarification hints.
items.title: Return clarification_required when required operational context is missing

items.body: The product shall deny private team exfiltration, hidden team access, raw workspace export, arbitrary backend execution, workflow bypass, and unapproved mutation attempts. Governed provider controls may be supported only when they remain bounded and cannot override team scope, approval policy, or export restrictions.
items.rationale: The source spec is explicit that backend options must stay bounded and that exfiltration, bypass, and raw export are denied behaviors.
items.title: Prevent hidden-team access, raw export, and unapproved mutation paths

items.body: The product shall enforce actor-aware visibility and team allowlists across search, preparation, and request flows. Responses shall expose only the level of detail appropriate for the caller's allowed team scope and governance posture.
items.rationale: The service boundary explicitly includes team allowlists and actor-aware visibility, though exact response-shaping rules are not yet fully specified.
items.title: Apply actor-aware visibility and team allowlist controls across all capabilities

items.body: The product shall maintain approval records and audit evidence for governed actions so reviewers can understand what was requested, why it was requested, what policy boundary applied, and whether the action was allowed, restricted, denied, or stopped for approval.
items.rationale: Audit and approval records are named in the service boundary and are important to the governance value proposition, though exact fields are not yet defined.
items.title: Preserve approval records and auditability for governed Linear actions

questions_for_user: Should issue creation preview also support priority as an explicit optional input, since the source spec mentions it but the starter operation schema does not?

envelope.title: Requirement Proposal: Linear Governed Fronting Showcase
watchouts: The source describes preview and request flows only; it does not define final mutation execution requirements.

There is a mismatch between the source spec and starter schema on optional inputs: search mentions project_id and cycle_id in the spec but not in the operation schema, and issue preparation mentions priority in the spec but not in the operation schema.
The cycle move summary mentions moving into a cycle or project, but the defined inputs only support target_cycle.
Approval, allowlist, actor visibility, and audit are core product behaviors and should not be left as implementation-only details.
backend_options must remain tightly bounded and must not become a path to arbitrary GraphQL execution, export, or hidden team access.

# Source 2: Linear fronting source specification
Kind: Fronting intent
Filename: source-spec.md

# Linear Governed Fronting Showcase Source Specification

This source document models a realistic fronting use case: Linear GraphQL API exists downstream, but agents should only see governed product-work capabilities.

## Purpose

Demonstrate that ANIP can govern issue-tracking behavior without exposing raw workspace tools directly to agents.

The ANIP contract owns the capability semantics. The Linear GraphQL API is the backend for this showcase. Linear MCP may be useful as a comparison point for raw tool exposure, but this package does not use MCP as the ANIP execution interface.

## Service Boundary

- Service ID: `linear-governance-service`
- Service name: Linear Governance Service
- Primary backend: native Linear GraphQL API adapter
- Deployment posture: centralized ANIP fronting service with team allowlists, actor-aware visibility, approval records, and audit.

## Backend Evidence

Native Linear API supply:

- Issue, project, team, label, cycle, and workflow search through GraphQL.
- Issue creation preview mapped to issue create mutation inputs.
- Comment preview mapped to comment create mutation inputs.
- Status transition request mapped to issue update workflow state.
- Cycle/project move request mapped to issue update mutation inputs.

MCP comparison:

- Linear MCP may expose issue search, issue creation, comments, and workflow updates.
- This showcase intentionally does not bind ANIP capabilities to Linear MCP tools. The agent-facing surface remains ANIP capabilities backed by bounded GraphQL operations.

## Governed Capability Surface

| Capability | User intent | Required inputs | Optional governed inputs | Outcome posture |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `linear.issue.search_context` | Search bounded Linear issues, projects, and cycles. | `team_key`, `query` | `project_id`, `cycle_id`, `limit`, `backend_options` | Read-only. Actor-visible summaries only. |
| `linear.issue.prepare` | Prepare an issue with team, labels, and priority. | `team_key`, `title`, `description` | `project_id`, `labels`, `priority`, `backend_options` | Preview-only. Requires approval before creation. |
| `linear.comment.prepare` | Prepare an issue comment. | `issue_id`, `comment_purpose`, `context` | `backend_options` | Preview-only. Requires approval before posting. |
| `linear.status_transition.request` | Request a status transition. | `issue_id`, `target_status`, `reason` | `backend_options` | Approval-gated. Restricted workflow states require explicit grant. |
| `linear.cycle_move.request` | Request moving an issue into a cycle. | `issue_id`, `target_cycle`, `reason` | `backend_options` | Approval-gated. Cross-team moves require explicit grant. |

`backend_options` is limited to bounded provider controls such as selected fields, pagination, or safe include flags. It must not allow arbitrary GraphQL execution, raw workspace export, or hidden team access.

## Policy Semantics

- Searches are bounded by team scope and result limits.
- Missing team, issue, target status, or target container context returns `clarification_required`.
- Issue creation, comments, transitions, and moves are preview or approval-gated flows.
- Private team exfiltration, workflow bypass, unapproved mutation, and raw exports are denied.
- Native GraphQL is the execution binding for this package. MCP can be used as a comparison surface, but is not required for ANIP.

## Why ANIP Helps

Linear GraphQL access provides powerful operations. ANIP exposes the organization-approved ways of using those operations, with explicit clarification, approval, restriction, denial, and audit semantics.

data.approval_posture_summary: Read-only search can proceed within bounded team scope. Issue creation and comment flows are preview-only and require approval before execution. Status transitions and cycle moves are approval-gated requests, with additional explicit grants required for restricted workflow states or cross-team moves.
business_goals: Enable bounded search across Linear issues, projects, and cycles within team scope.
Let users prepare issue creation and comment drafts before anything is posted or created.
Gate status transitions and cycle moves behind explicit approval-aware request flows.
Prevent private team exfiltration, workflow bypass, unapproved mutation, and raw exports.
Maintain actor-aware visibility, approval records, and audit across the fronting service.
data.business_problem: Raw Linear API access is powerful but too open for agent-facing use. The business needs organization-approved issue-tracking workflows that preserve team scope, actor-aware visibility, approval controls, and audit instead of exposing unrestricted workspace operations.
data.governed_behavior_summary: The product returns bounded, actor-visible Linear results, asks for clarification when required context is missing, prepares previews instead of executing write actions directly, and denies attempts to bypass team scope, approval policy, workflow restrictions, or export controls.
data.product_purpose: Provide a governed Linear fronting experience that lets users discover issue context and prepare issue, comment, status-change, and cycle-move actions without exposing raw workspace tools.

Missing team, issue, target status, or target container context should trigger clarification rather than silent execution.

data.policy_summary: Linear Governance Service should expose only governed product-work capabilities: team-scoped issue discovery returns bounded actor-visible summaries; issue and comment actions are preview-only; status transitions and cycle moves are approval-gated requests; missing required context yields clarification stops; raw exports, hidden team access, workflow bypass, and unapproved mutations are denied.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow team-scoped issue, project, and cycle discovery as bounded, actor-visible summaries only, subject to team scope and result limits.
rules.notes: Matches read-only search posture and bounded result semantics in the brief.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of an issue creation preview with team, project, labels, and related governed inputs, but stop for approval before any issue is created.
rules.notes: Covers preview-only issue preparation rather than direct mutation.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of an issue comment preview from bounded context, but stop for approval before posting the comment.
rules.notes: Keeps comment handling in a governed preview flow.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of status-transition and cycle-move requests with business justification, but stop for approval before any workflow change is executed.
rules.notes: Applies to governed workflow change requests rather than direct updates.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow only governed previews for workflow change requests; restricted workflow states and cross-team move scenarios must not be completed directly and should surface as restricted or escalated outcomes.
rules.notes: The source mentions explicit grant requirements for restricted states and cross-team moves, but does not define final grant mechanics.

rules.governed_outcome: Deny requests for arbitrary GraphQL execution, raw workspace export, hidden team access, workflow bypass, private team exfiltration, or unapproved mutations.
rules.notes: Represents the hard governance boundary of the fronting service.

rules.governed_outcome: When required team or query context is missing or ambiguous, stop and request clarification before searching.
rules.notes: Clarification behavior is part of the governed outcome model, not an implementation detail.

rules.governed_outcome: When issue, target status, target cycle, or other required workflow-change context is missing or ambiguous, stop and request clarification before preparing the request.
rules.notes: Applies to request-stage workflow changes only.

entries.statement: Do not expose raw Linear GraphQL operations or raw workspace tooling directly to agents.

entries.rationale: Issue and comment flows are preview-only, while status transitions and cycle moves are approval-gated requests.

entries.rationale: The brief limits backend_options to bounded provider controls and explicitly forbids arbitrary GraphQL, hidden team access, and raw export behavior.
entries.statement: Do not support arbitrary GraphQL execution, unrestricted provider controls, or raw workspace export through backend options.

entries.rationale: Search is bounded by team scope, private team exfiltration is denied, and deployment posture includes team allowlists and actor-aware visibility.
entries.statement: Do not provide cross-team or private-team access beyond team-scoped allowlists and actor-visible summaries.

entries.rationale: The defined capability surface is limited to issue discovery, issue preparation, comment preparation, status transition requests, and cycle move requests.
entries.statement: Do not position this showcase as a full Linear administration surface for unrestricted workflow or workspace management.

entries.evidence: Scenario review shows search requests require team context when missing, enforce result limits and team scope, and return actor-visible summaries rather than raw Linear tool access.
entries.statement: Team-scoped users can search Linear issue context through the governed fronting service and receive bounded, actor-visible issue, project, or cycle results without exposure to raw workspace tools.

entries.evidence: Representative issue and comment scenarios show the service gathers required inputs, returns a preview payload, and clearly indicates that approval is required before creation or posting.
entries.statement: Issue preparation and comment preparation produce usable previews for review while stopping before any unapproved Linear mutation occurs.

entries.evidence: Review scenarios show transition and move requests generate approval-ready previews, return clarification when target context is missing, and block restricted state changes or cross-team moves without the required grant.
entries.statement: Status transitions and cycle moves are handled as governed requests that stop for approval, with restricted workflow states and cross-team moves prevented unless explicitly granted.

entries.evidence: PM review of representative flows shows missing team, issue, status, or target container context returns clarification-required, while private team access, workflow bypass, unapproved mutation, and raw export attempts are explicitly denied or restricted.
entries.statement: Governance boundaries are visible and reviewable: the service surfaces clarification-required, approval-required, restricted, or denied outcomes instead of silently bypassing policy.

entries.evidence: Design or operational review can point to approval records, actor-aware result visibility, and audit signals for representative search and request flows.

data.approval_posture_summary: Read-only search can proceed within bounded team scope. Issue creation and comment flows are preview-only and require approval before execution. Status transitions and cycle moves are approval-gated requests, with additional explicit grants required for restricted workflow states or cross-team moves.
business_goals: Enable bounded search across Linear issues, projects, and cycles within team scope.
Let users prepare issue creation and comment drafts before anything is posted or created.
Gate status transitions and cycle moves behind explicit approval-aware request flows.
Prevent private team exfiltration, workflow bypass, unapproved mutation, and raw exports.
Maintain actor-aware visibility, approval records, and audit across the fronting service.
data.business_problem: Raw Linear API access is powerful but too open for agent-facing use. The business needs organization-approved issue-tracking workflows that preserve team scope, actor-aware visibility, approval controls, and audit instead of exposing unrestricted workspace operations.
data.governed_behavior_summary: The product returns bounded, actor-visible Linear results, asks for clarification when required context is missing, prepares previews instead of executing write actions directly, and denies attempts to bypass team scope, approval policy, workflow restrictions, or export controls.
data.product_purpose: Provide a governed Linear fronting experience that lets users discover issue context and prepare issue, comment, status-change, and cycle-move actions without exposing raw workspace tools.

items.body: For linear.issue.search_context, linear.issue.prepare, linear.comment.prepare, linear.status_transition.request, and linear.cycle_move.request, require runtime evaluation of actor authority against team allowlist and actor-visible scope before backend execution or response shaping. Search must stay within the requested allowed team. Preparation and request flows must not reveal hidden-team issue, cycle, project, or workflow details beyond the actor's permitted visibility. When the actor lacks access to the referenced team or issue context, return restricted or denied according to the chosen caller-visible policy rather than exposing raw backend errors or hidden metadata.
items.rationale: The locked baseline explicitly requires actor-aware visibility and team allowlist controls across search, preparation, and request flows, and forbids hidden-team access and raw export behavior.
items.title: Bind actor-aware team scope and visibility across all Linear capabilities

items.body: At runtime, produce clarification_required instead of guessing when required context is missing or ambiguous. Preserve explicit clarification stops for: team_key and query on linear.issue.search_context; team_key, title, and description on linear.issue.prepare; issue_id, comment_purpose, and context on linear.comment.prepare; issue_id, target_status, and reason on linear.status_transition.request; and issue_id, target_cycle, and reason on linear.cycle_move.request. Also use clarification_required when issue reference, target status, target cycle, or team scope cannot be resolved unambiguously from caller input.
items.rationale: The baseline repeatedly requires explicit clarification semantics and the source operation contracts already define required inputs and clarification hints.
items.title: Bind clarification_required to missing or ambiguous operational context

items.body: Treat linear.issue.prepare, linear.comment.prepare, linear.status_transition.request, and linear.cycle_move.request as write-adjacent but non-mutating runtime actions. Runtime policy must allow these capabilities to prepare a preview or governed request artifact only, and must block any direct issue creation, comment posting, status update, or cycle reassignment from the fronting surface. Responses should make the non-executing posture explicit so callers understand that preparation succeeded but no downstream mutation has occurred.
items.rationale: The baseline requires preview-first behavior for issue and comment creation flows and approval-gated request semantics for status transitions and cycle moves, with no direct mutation path exposed.
items.title: Bind write-adjacent capabilities to preview or request artifacts only

items.body: After successful validation and policy checks, linear.issue.prepare, linear.comment.prepare, linear.status_transition.request, and linear.cycle_move.request must stop at an approval boundary and return an approval-required style outcome with the prepared artifact, policy boundary, and captured business context. Runtime policy should preserve the distinction between preparation/request generation and any later approved execution path. No downstream mutation may proceed without explicit approval binding defined outside this fronting request.
items.rationale: The baseline states that all write-adjacent Linear actions are approval-gated and must stop at a preview or request artifact before any mutation is allowed.
items.title: Bind approval-required outcomes for all governed write-adjacent flows

items.body: For linear.status_transition.request, require runtime evaluation of whether the target workflow state is restricted for the actor; if so, return restricted unless an explicit grant is present. For linear.cycle_move.request, detect whether the requested move crosses team boundaries and return restricted unless an explicit grant is present. These controls must be enforced before approval handoff so approval does not become a bypass for restricted-state or cross-team policy.
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires restricted workflow state controls for status transitions and explicit grant requirements for cross-team cycle moves.
items.title: Bind restricted controls for workflow states and cross-team cycle moves

items.body: For linear.issue.search_context, enforce read-only execution, required team scoping, bounded result counts, and actor-visible summaries only. Runtime policy should treat limit as optional with the documented default when omitted, but must still enforce a bounded maximum to prevent raw workspace export behavior. backend_options may be passed only when explicitly supplied and must be filtered so they cannot expand scope, expose hidden fields, or create export-like responses.
items.rationale: The baseline requires bounded team-scoped issue discovery, denies raw export and hidden-team access, and the source operation contract defines read-only search with optional limit and bounded provider options.
items.title: Bind bounded search execution and anti-export controls

items.body: For every capability invocation, capture durable and searchable audit records with task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id when present. For governed write-adjacent flows, record who requested the action, what was requested, the stated reason or purpose, the evaluated policy boundary, and whether the outcome was clarification_required, approval_required, restricted, denied, or allowed for preview generation. Audit records should preserve enough evidence for later review without exposing hidden-team data to unauthorized callers.
items.rationale: The baseline requires durable searchable audit, explicit lineage fields, and preserved approval records and audit evidence for governed Linear actions.
items.title: Bind durable audit and lineage capture to governed outcomes

Should restricted and denied outcomes expose the same explanation detail to callers, or should restricted responses be more masked?
Do you want an explicit max limit policy for search results, or should runtime only preserve the documented default of 20 when limit is omitted?

watchouts: The baseline requires approval-gated preparation and request flows, but it does not define the concrete approver identity or approval transport.
Cross-team move restrictions are explicit for cycle moves, but the exact runtime signal for detecting cross-team context is not yet bound.
Restricted versus denied is required by the baseline, but the caller-visible distinction needs explicit runtime semantics.
backend_options are allowed only as bounded provider controls and must never bypass team scope, approval policy, or export restrictions.
Search is read-only, but actor-aware visibility still requires result shaping to avoid hidden-team leakage or raw workspace export.

items.body: Define a shared backend binding for the Linear fronting service that targets the native API connection reference `linear_fronting`. Map the canonical capabilities to their explicit operation paths only: `linear.issue.search_context` -> `/linear/issue_search_context`, `linear.issue.prepare` -> `/linear/issue_prepare`, `linear.comment.prepare` -> `/linear/comment_prepare`, `linear.status_transition.request` -> `/linear/status_transition_request`, and `linear.cycle_move.request` -> `/linear/cycle_move_request`. Treat these paths as the only allowed backend entrypoints for this service.
items.rationale: The source backend starter provides one connection reference, one backend kind, and explicit per-capability paths for all locked capabilities.
items.title: Bind all canonical capabilities to the governed Linear native API connection

items.body: Bind each capability using explicit input mode only. Forward only the documented inputs for that capability, and require clarification before invocation when required fields are missing or ambiguous. Preserve entity-reference semantics for `team_key`, `issue_id`, `project_id`, and `target_cycle`, and omit optional fields unless explicitly supplied. Do not infer missing operational context from prior calls or hidden backend state.
items.rationale: The starter spec marks every operation as `backend_input_mode: explicit`, and the locked baseline requires clarification-required behavior for missing or ambiguous context.
items.title: Use explicit per-capability input forwarding with clarification stops

items.body: Implement `linear.issue.search_context` as the only read-executing backend binding. Implement `linear.issue.prepare`, `linear.comment.prepare`, `linear.status_transition.request`, and `linear.cycle_move.request` as write-adjacent bindings that generate governed previews or request artifacts only. These bindings may validate references and assemble preview payloads through the adapter, but they must not perform downstream Linear mutations in the same execution path.
items.rationale: The source marks search as `read` and the other four operations as `write_adjacent`, while the locked baseline requires preview-first and approval-gated behavior.
items.title: Separate read execution from write-adjacent preview execution

items.body: Insert a policy gate in front of every backend call to enforce actor-aware visibility, team allowlists, restricted-vs-denied semantics, and purpose-bound authority. For search, verify allowed team scope before querying. For status transition and cycle move requests, evaluate restricted workflow-state and cross-team grant requirements before preparing the request. Return governed outcomes such as `clarification_required`, `restricted`, or `denied` without calling the backend when policy prerequisites are not met.
items.rationale: The locked baseline makes policy enforcement central to all capabilities and explicitly calls out restricted workflow states, cross-team move controls, and actor-aware visibility.
items.title: Apply governance and scope checks before backend adapter invocation

items.body: Support `backend_options` as an optional bounded object on all five bindings, but validate it against an explicit allowlist before forwarding anything to the adapter. Permit only provider controls consistent with bounded field selection or pagination. Reject or strip any option that could expand team scope, expose raw exports, bypass approval boundaries, or enable arbitrary backend behavior.
items.rationale: The starter spec includes `backend_options` on every operation and explicitly states that it must not bypass team scope, approval, or export policy.
items.title: Constrain backend_options with an allowlisted pass-through policy

items.body: Wrap each backend binding in a standard audit envelope that records actor identity, capability id, input summary, policy outcome, approval stop state, and backend operation reference. Include the required lineage fields from the locked baseline: `task_id`, `invocation_id`, `client_reference_id`, and `parent_invocation_id` when present. Persist enough detail to support searchable audit review without storing raw hidden-team data unnecessarily.
items.rationale: The baseline requires durable searchable auditability and explicitly names the lineage fields that must be preserved.
items.title: Attach durable audit and lineage metadata to every backend interaction

items.body: Bind adapter outputs to governed response models per capability: bounded issue results for search, issue creation preview for issue preparation, comment preview for comment preparation, status transition preview for workflow changes, and cycle move preview for cycle reassignment. Mask or omit backend-native fields that exceed actor visibility or expose raw workspace structure. Keep requester feedback explicit when an action is stopped for approval, restricted, or denied.
items.rationale: The source operation starter defines output intent per capability, and the locked baseline prohibits raw workspace exposure while requiring actor-aware result shaping.
items.title: Shape backend responses into governed summaries rather than raw provider payloads

questions_for_user: Should all five capabilities bind through the same Linear connection reference, or do any require separate credentials or environment routing?
What concrete environment label should the binding target first: production, staging, or sandbox?
Should backend_options be passed through as a bounded allowlisted object, or should the service strip unsupported provider fields before adapter invocation?
For approval-gated flows, should the backend binding stop at preview generation only, or also emit a separate approval-request artifact to another internal system?
What durable audit sink should capture request, policy outcome, and lineage metadata for these bindings?
envelope.summary: Candidate backend-binding blocks for the Linear fronting service, grounded in the locked capability inventory and starter backend operation spec. These blocks focus on explicit binding to the governed Linear native API surface, approval-gated write-adjacent flows, and audit-friendly request handling.
envelope.title: Backend Binding Proposal: Linear Fronting Showcase
watchouts: The locked baseline allows only the five canonical capabilities; backend bindings must not introduce raw workspace tools or arbitrary GraphQL execution.
Issue preparation, comment preparation, status transition requests, and cycle move requests are write-adjacent only and must stop before mutation.
Actor-aware visibility and team allowlist controls must be enforced before backend invocation and again when shaping returned data.
backend_options are explicitly bounded and must not bypass team scope, approval policy, or export restrictions.
Clarification-required behavior is part of the contract; missing team, issue, target status, target cycle, or reason must not be guessed.
Cross-team cycle moves and restricted workflow states require explicit grant handling and should surface restricted outcomes cleanly.

next_steps: Review and accept, edit, or reject the candidate verification blocks individually.
Decide whether audit and lineage evidence must be asserted at response-contract level or only in downstream logs and records.
Confirm whether actor-differentiated verification should be mandatory for both status transition and cycle move flows.

items.body: Verification should prove that issue context search requires team_key and query, returns clarification_required when either is missing or ambiguous, constrains execution to the caller's allowed team scope, and returns bounded results rather than raw workspace data. Evidence should include positive search cases within an allowed team, negative cases for hidden or disallowed team access, result-limit enforcement, and response shaping that exposes only actor-visible summaries.
items.rationale: The locked baseline and source operation contract explicitly require team-scoped bounded search, clarification semantics, actor-aware visibility, and denial of hidden-team access or raw export.
items.title: Verify bounded team-scoped issue context search behavior

items.body: Verification should prove that issue preparation and comment preparation collect all required inputs, stop at preview generation, and do not create issues or post comments directly. Evidence should include required-input clarification tests, preview artifact generation for valid requests, explicit approval-stop signaling, and negative checks showing no downstream mutation occurs without a separate approved path. Optional fields such as project, labels, and bounded provider options should be verified as additive only and not required for baseline success.
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly defines issue and comment flows as preparation-only, approval-gated, and distinct from execution.
items.title: Verify preview-first preparation for issue and comment flows

items.body: Verification should prove that status transition requests require issue_id, target_status, and business reason; return clarification_required when required context is missing or ambiguous; prepare a governed request instead of changing workflow state directly; and stop for approval before execution. Tests should also prove restricted-state handling by showing that actors without the needed grant receive restricted or denied outcomes rather than workflow bypass. Evidence should make the policy reason for the outcome inspectable.
items.rationale: Status transition governance, reason capture, restricted-state controls, and approval gating are directly stated in the locked requirements and scenario set.
items.title: Verify governed status transition request controls

items.body: Verification should prove that cycle move requests require issue_id, target_cycle, and business reason; return clarification_required when issue or target cycle context is missing or ambiguous; prepare a move request rather than moving the issue directly; and stop for approval before execution. Tests should explicitly cover cross-team movement restrictions, including proof that moves crossing team boundaries are restricted unless an explicit grant is present, and that requester feedback remains clear when the move cannot proceed.
items.rationale: The baseline and scenario text explicitly call out approval-gated cycle moves, required reason capture, and explicit grant requirements for cross-team moves.
items.title: Verify governed cycle move request controls

items.body: Verification should include paired scenarios where two actors submit the same status transition or cycle move request and receive different governed outcomes based on authority, team scope, or workflow restrictions. Evidence should show that a permitted actor can receive a prepared request that still stops for approval, while a narrower actor receives a restricted, masked, or denied result. The verification record should make the reason for the difference explicit without leaking hidden-team or restricted workflow details.
items.rationale: Actor-aware visibility and authority are baseline requirements, and one scenario explicitly frames same-request different-outcome behavior, though the exact canonical flow is less concrete than the primary capability scenarios.
items.title: Verify actor-aware visibility and authority differences on the same request

items.body: Verification should prove that governed Linear actions preserve durable, searchable audit evidence and approval records showing what was requested, why it was requested, what policy boundary applied, and whether the result was allowed, restricted, denied, clarification_required, or stopped for approval. Evidence should also confirm lineage capture for task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id where applicable. Coverage should include read and write-adjacent flows so audit posture is not limited to successful requests only.
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires durable searchable audit, approval records, and lineage fields, but the exact implementation surface for these records is not fully specified in the provided source.
items.title: Verify auditability, approval records, and lineage across governed outcomes

items.body: Verification should prove that the service exposes only the approved Linear capability surface and does not permit raw workspace tooling, arbitrary backend execution, hidden-team exfiltration, workflow bypass, unapproved mutation attempts, or provider options that override governance controls. Evidence should include negative tests against unsupported operations, attempts to over-broaden backend options, and confirmation that restricted_vs_denied semantics are used instead of silently executing disallowed requests.
items.rationale: Preventing raw tool exposure, export behavior, hidden-team access, arbitrary backend execution, and unapproved mutation is a core baseline requirement and central to the fronting-service intent.
items.title: Verify denial of raw workspace access and policy bypass paths

questions_for_user: Should verification expectations require negative tests for every capability, or only for write-adjacent and restricted flows?
Do you want approval-stop verification to require a concrete approval artifact shape, or only proof that execution does not occur before approval?
Should audit verification require lineage fields on every response path, including clarification_required, restricted, and denied outcomes?
envelope.summary: Candidate developer-facing verification expectation blocks derived from the locked Linear fronting baseline. Focus is on proving preview-first governance, clarification behavior, actor-aware restrictions, bounded search, and auditability across the canonical capability set.
envelope.title: Verification Expectations Proposal: Linear Fronting Showcase
watchouts: The baseline is strong on governed outcomes and approval stops, but it does not define exact payload schemas for approval artifacts or audit records.
Search is read-only, but verification still needs to prove bounded results, actor-visible summaries, and no raw workspace export behavior.
Write-adjacent capabilities must be verified as preview or request preparation only; tests should not assume direct mutation paths exist.
Restricted versus denied semantics are required by the baseline, but the exact boundary between them may need explicit product clarification.
Optional backend options are allowed only when bounded; verification should ensure they cannot override team scope, approval policy, or export restrictions.

data.summary: Prepare a governed request to move a Linear issue into a cycle or project and stop for approval.
data.title: Request Cycle Move

accepted_payload.body: For linear.issue.search_context, linear.issue.prepare, linear.comment.prepare, linear.status_transition.request, and linear.cycle_move.request, require runtime evaluation of actor authority against team allowlist and actor-visible scope before backend execution or response shaping. Search must stay within the requested allowed team. Preparation and request flows must not reveal hidden-team issue, cycle, project, or workflow details beyond the actor's permitted visibility. When the actor lacks access to the referenced team or issue context, return restricted or denied according to the chosen caller-visible policy rather than exposing raw backend errors or hidden metadata.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline explicitly requires actor-aware visibility and team allowlist controls across search, preparation, and request flows, and forbids hidden-team access and raw export behavior.
accepted_payload.title: Bind actor-aware team scope and visibility across all Linear capabilities

accepted_payload.body: At runtime, produce clarification_required instead of guessing when required context is missing or ambiguous. Preserve explicit clarification stops for: team_key and query on linear.issue.search_context; team_key, title, and description on linear.issue.prepare; issue_id, comment_purpose, and context on linear.comment.prepare; issue_id, target_status, and reason on linear.status_transition.request; and issue_id, target_cycle, and reason on linear.cycle_move.request. Also use clarification_required when issue reference, target status, target cycle, or team scope cannot be resolved unambiguously from caller input.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline repeatedly requires explicit clarification semantics and the source operation contracts already define required inputs and clarification hints.
accepted_payload.title: Bind clarification_required to missing or ambiguous operational context

accepted_payload.body: Treat linear.issue.prepare, linear.comment.prepare, linear.status_transition.request, and linear.cycle_move.request as write-adjacent but non-mutating runtime actions. Runtime policy must allow these capabilities to prepare a preview or governed request artifact only, and must block any direct issue creation, comment posting, status update, or cycle reassignment from the fronting surface. Responses should make the non-executing posture explicit so callers understand that preparation succeeded but no downstream mutation has occurred.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline requires preview-first behavior for issue and comment creation flows and approval-gated request semantics for status transitions and cycle moves, with no direct mutation path exposed.
accepted_payload.title: Bind write-adjacent capabilities to preview or request artifacts only

accepted_payload.body: After successful validation and policy checks, linear.issue.prepare, linear.comment.prepare, linear.status_transition.request, and linear.cycle_move.request must stop at an approval boundary and return an approval-required style outcome with the prepared artifact, policy boundary, and captured business context. Runtime policy should preserve the distinction between preparation/request generation and any later approved execution path. No downstream mutation may proceed without explicit approval binding defined outside this fronting request.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline states that all write-adjacent Linear actions are approval-gated and must stop at a preview or request artifact before any mutation is allowed.
accepted_payload.title: Bind approval-required outcomes for all governed write-adjacent flows

accepted_payload.body: For linear.status_transition.request, require runtime evaluation of whether the target workflow state is restricted for the actor; if so, return restricted unless an explicit grant is present. For linear.cycle_move.request, detect whether the requested move crosses team boundaries and return restricted unless an explicit grant is present. These controls must be enforced before approval handoff so approval does not become a bypass for restricted-state or cross-team policy.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires restricted workflow state controls for status transitions and explicit grant requirements for cross-team cycle moves.
accepted_payload.title: Bind restricted controls for workflow states and cross-team cycle moves

accepted_payload.body: For linear.issue.search_context, enforce read-only execution, required team scoping, bounded result counts, and actor-visible summaries only. Runtime policy should treat limit as optional with the documented default when omitted, but must still enforce a bounded maximum to prevent raw workspace export behavior. backend_options may be passed only when explicitly supplied and must be filtered so they cannot expand scope, expose hidden fields, or create export-like responses.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline requires bounded team-scoped issue discovery, denies raw export and hidden-team access, and the source operation contract defines read-only search with optional limit and bounded provider options.
accepted_payload.title: Bind bounded search execution and anti-export controls

accepted_payload.body: For every capability invocation, capture durable and searchable audit records with task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id when present. For governed write-adjacent flows, record who requested the action, what was requested, the stated reason or purpose, the evaluated policy boundary, and whether the outcome was clarification_required, approval_required, restricted, denied, or allowed for preview generation. Audit records should preserve enough evidence for later review without exposing hidden-team data to unauthorized callers.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline requires durable searchable audit, explicit lineage fields, and preserved approval records and audit evidence for governed Linear actions.
accepted_payload.title: Bind durable audit and lineage capture to governed outcomes

pm_artifacts.title: Runtime Policy Binding Proposal: Linear Fronting Showcase

accepted_payload.body: Define a shared backend binding for the Linear fronting service that targets the native API connection reference `linear_fronting`. Map the canonical capabilities to their explicit operation paths only: `linear.issue.search_context` -> `/linear/issue_search_context`, `linear.issue.prepare` -> `/linear/issue_prepare`, `linear.comment.prepare` -> `/linear/comment_prepare`, `linear.status_transition.request` -> `/linear/status_transition_request`, and `linear.cycle_move.request` -> `/linear/cycle_move_request`. Treat these paths as the only allowed backend entrypoints for this service.
accepted_payload.rationale: The source backend starter provides one connection reference, one backend kind, and explicit per-capability paths for all locked capabilities.
accepted_payload.title: Bind all canonical capabilities to the governed Linear native API connection

accepted_payload.body: Bind each capability using explicit input mode only. Forward only the documented inputs for that capability, and require clarification before invocation when required fields are missing or ambiguous. Preserve entity-reference semantics for `team_key`, `issue_id`, `project_id`, and `target_cycle`, and omit optional fields unless explicitly supplied. Do not infer missing operational context from prior calls or hidden backend state.
accepted_payload.rationale: The starter spec marks every operation as `backend_input_mode: explicit`, and the locked baseline requires clarification-required behavior for missing or ambiguous context.
accepted_payload.title: Use explicit per-capability input forwarding with clarification stops

accepted_payload.body: Implement `linear.issue.search_context` as the only read-executing backend binding. Implement `linear.issue.prepare`, `linear.comment.prepare`, `linear.status_transition.request`, and `linear.cycle_move.request` as write-adjacent bindings that generate governed previews or request artifacts only. These bindings may validate references and assemble preview payloads through the adapter, but they must not perform downstream Linear mutations in the same execution path.
accepted_payload.rationale: The source marks search as `read` and the other four operations as `write_adjacent`, while the locked baseline requires preview-first and approval-gated behavior.
accepted_payload.title: Separate read execution from write-adjacent preview execution

accepted_payload.body: Insert a policy gate in front of every backend call to enforce actor-aware visibility, team allowlists, restricted-vs-denied semantics, and purpose-bound authority. For search, verify allowed team scope before querying. For status transition and cycle move requests, evaluate restricted workflow-state and cross-team grant requirements before preparing the request. Return governed outcomes such as `clarification_required`, `restricted`, or `denied` without calling the backend when policy prerequisites are not met.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline makes policy enforcement central to all capabilities and explicitly calls out restricted workflow states, cross-team move controls, and actor-aware visibility.
accepted_payload.title: Apply governance and scope checks before backend adapter invocation

accepted_payload.body: Support `backend_options` as an optional bounded object on all five bindings, but validate it against an explicit allowlist before forwarding anything to the adapter. Permit only provider controls consistent with bounded field selection or pagination. Reject or strip any option that could expand team scope, expose raw exports, bypass approval boundaries, or enable arbitrary backend behavior.
accepted_payload.rationale: The starter spec includes `backend_options` on every operation and explicitly states that it must not bypass team scope, approval, or export policy.
accepted_payload.title: Constrain backend_options with an allowlisted pass-through policy

accepted_payload.body: Wrap each backend binding in a standard audit envelope that records actor identity, capability id, input summary, policy outcome, approval stop state, and backend operation reference. Include the required lineage fields from the locked baseline: `task_id`, `invocation_id`, `client_reference_id`, and `parent_invocation_id` when present. Persist enough detail to support searchable audit review without storing raw hidden-team data unnecessarily.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline requires durable searchable auditability and explicitly names the lineage fields that must be preserved.
accepted_payload.title: Attach durable audit and lineage metadata to every backend interaction

accepted_payload.body: Bind adapter outputs to governed response models per capability: bounded issue results for search, issue creation preview for issue preparation, comment preview for comment preparation, status transition preview for workflow changes, and cycle move preview for cycle reassignment. Mask or omit backend-native fields that exceed actor visibility or expose raw workspace structure. Keep requester feedback explicit when an action is stopped for approval, restricted, or denied.
accepted_payload.rationale: The source operation starter defines output intent per capability, and the locked baseline prohibits raw workspace exposure while requiring actor-aware result shaping.
accepted_payload.title: Shape backend responses into governed summaries rather than raw provider payloads

pm_artifacts.title: Backend Binding Proposal: Linear Fronting Showcase

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that issue context search requires team_key and query, returns clarification_required when either is missing or ambiguous, constrains execution to the caller's allowed team scope, and returns bounded results rather than raw workspace data. Evidence should include positive search cases within an allowed team, negative cases for hidden or disallowed team access, result-limit enforcement, and response shaping that exposes only actor-visible summaries.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline and source operation contract explicitly require team-scoped bounded search, clarification semantics, actor-aware visibility, and denial of hidden-team access or raw export.
accepted_payload.title: Verify bounded team-scoped issue context search behavior

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that issue preparation and comment preparation collect all required inputs, stop at preview generation, and do not create issues or post comments directly. Evidence should include required-input clarification tests, preview artifact generation for valid requests, explicit approval-stop signaling, and negative checks showing no downstream mutation occurs without a separate approved path. Optional fields such as project, labels, and bounded provider options should be verified as additive only and not required for baseline success.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline explicitly defines issue and comment flows as preparation-only, approval-gated, and distinct from execution.
accepted_payload.title: Verify preview-first preparation for issue and comment flows

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that status transition requests require issue_id, target_status, and business reason; return clarification_required when required context is missing or ambiguous; prepare a governed request instead of changing workflow state directly; and stop for approval before execution. Tests should also prove restricted-state handling by showing that actors without the needed grant receive restricted or denied outcomes rather than workflow bypass. Evidence should make the policy reason for the outcome inspectable.
accepted_payload.rationale: Status transition governance, reason capture, restricted-state controls, and approval gating are directly stated in the locked requirements and scenario set.
accepted_payload.title: Verify governed status transition request controls

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that cycle move requests require issue_id, target_cycle, and business reason; return clarification_required when issue or target cycle context is missing or ambiguous; prepare a move request rather than moving the issue directly; and stop for approval before execution. Tests should explicitly cover cross-team movement restrictions, including proof that moves crossing team boundaries are restricted unless an explicit grant is present, and that requester feedback remains clear when the move cannot proceed.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline and scenario text explicitly call out approval-gated cycle moves, required reason capture, and explicit grant requirements for cross-team moves.
accepted_payload.title: Verify governed cycle move request controls

accepted_payload.body: Verification should include paired scenarios where two actors submit the same status transition or cycle move request and receive different governed outcomes based on authority, team scope, or workflow restrictions. Evidence should show that a permitted actor can receive a prepared request that still stops for approval, while a narrower actor receives a restricted, masked, or denied result. The verification record should make the reason for the difference explicit without leaking hidden-team or restricted workflow details.
accepted_payload.rationale: Actor-aware visibility and authority are baseline requirements, and one scenario explicitly frames same-request different-outcome behavior, though the exact canonical flow is less concrete than the primary capability scenarios.
accepted_payload.title: Verify actor-aware visibility and authority differences on the same request

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that governed Linear actions preserve durable, searchable audit evidence and approval records showing what was requested, why it was requested, what policy boundary applied, and whether the result was allowed, restricted, denied, clarification_required, or stopped for approval. Evidence should also confirm lineage capture for task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id where applicable. Coverage should include read and write-adjacent flows so audit posture is not limited to successful requests only.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires durable searchable audit, approval records, and lineage fields, but the exact implementation surface for these records is not fully specified in the provided source.
accepted_payload.title: Verify auditability, approval records, and lineage across governed outcomes

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that the service exposes only the approved Linear capability surface and does not permit raw workspace tooling, arbitrary backend execution, hidden-team exfiltration, workflow bypass, unapproved mutation attempts, or provider options that override governance controls. Evidence should include negative tests against unsupported operations, attempts to over-broaden backend options, and confirmation that restricted_vs_denied semantics are used instead of silently executing disallowed requests.
accepted_payload.rationale: Preventing raw tool exposure, export behavior, hidden-team access, arbitrary backend execution, and unapproved mutation is a core baseline requirement and central to the fronting-service intent.
accepted_payload.title: Verify denial of raw workspace access and policy bypass paths

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can search issue, project, and cycle context; prepare issue creation previews; prepare comment previews; request status transitions; and request cycle or project moves through governed capability flows.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: May initiate preview or request flows but should not directly execute creation, posting, restricted transitions, or governed moves without approval. Missing team, issue, target status, or target container context should trigger clarification rather than silent execution.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Uses the governed Linear fronting service to search issue context and prepare issue, comment, status transition, or cycle move requests without direct raw workspace access.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see actor-visible summaries and bounded search results within team allowlists and result limits. Must not see hidden teams, raw workspace exports, or unrestricted GraphQL data.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can provide team-scoped search inputs and prepare work items tied to their allowed team context. Can request moves or transitions only within the governed scope presented by the service.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Does not gain approval authority merely by being team-scoped. Approval is still required for issue creation, comment posting, status transitions, and cycle or project moves where the brief marks them as gated.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: A requester operating within an allowed Linear team scope for bounded discovery and preparation workflows.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can access issue, project, and cycle summaries only for allowed teams and only within bounded result limits. Private team exfiltration and hidden team access are denied.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review issue creation previews, comment previews, status transition requests, and cycle or project move requests. May approve or deny according to governance rules, especially where the flow explicitly stops for approval.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Holds approval authority for at least some gated mutation flows, but the brief does not specify whether authority differs by action type, restricted workflow state, or cross-team move. Those boundaries should be confirmed before finalizing.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Reviews governed previews and requests before mutation actions proceed in Linear.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect the preview or request details, business reason, and approval record context needed to approve or deny governed actions. Broader data visibility beyond the requested item is not established by the brief.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: team_scoped_member bounded access to Linear Issue Discovery should return bounded_result. Allow team-scoped issue, project, and cycle discovery as bounded, actor-visible summaries only, subject to team scope and result limits. Matches read-only search posture and bounded result semantics in the brief.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow team-scoped issue, project, and cycle discovery as bounded, actor-visible summaries only, subject to team scope and result limits.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: linear_requester approval_required access to Linear Issue Preparation should return approval_stop. Allow preparation of an issue creation preview with team, project, labels, and related governed inputs, but stop for approval before any issue is created. Covers preview-only issue preparation rather than direct mutation.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of an issue creation preview with team, project, labels, and related governed inputs, but stop for approval before any issue is created.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: linear_requester approval_required access to Linear Comment Preparation should return approval_stop. Allow preparation of an issue comment preview from bounded context, but stop for approval before posting the comment. Keeps comment handling in a governed preview flow.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of an issue comment preview from bounded context, but stop for approval before posting the comment.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: linear_requester approval_required access to Linear Workflow Change Requests should return approval_stop. Allow preparation of status-transition and cycle-move requests with business justification, but stop for approval before any workflow change is executed. Applies to governed workflow change requests rather than direct updates.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of status-transition and cycle-move requests with business justification, but stop for approval before any workflow change is executed.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: team_scoped_member restricted access to Linear Workflow Change Requests should return masked_or_restricted_result. Allow only governed previews for workflow change requests; restricted workflow states and cross-team move scenarios must not be completed directly and should surface as restricted or escalated outcomes. The source mentions explicit grant requirements for restricted states and cross-team moves, but does not define final grant mechanics.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow only governed previews for workflow change requests; restricted workflow states and cross-team move scenarios must not be completed directly and should surface as restricted or escalated outcomes.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: linear_requester denied access to Linear Access Governance should return deny_request. Deny requests for arbitrary GraphQL execution, raw workspace export, hidden team access, workflow bypass, private team exfiltration, or unapproved mutations. Represents the hard governance boundary of the fronting service.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Deny requests for arbitrary GraphQL execution, raw workspace export, hidden team access, workflow bypass, private team exfiltration, or unapproved mutations.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: linear_requester bounded access to Linear Issue Discovery should return clarification_required. When required team or query context is missing or ambiguous, stop and request clarification before searching. Clarification behavior is part of the governed outcome model, not an implementation detail.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: When required team or query context is missing or ambiguous, stop and request clarification before searching.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: linear_requester bounded access to Linear Workflow Change Requests should return clarification_required. When issue, target status, target cycle, or other required workflow-change context is missing or ambiguous, stop and request clarification before preparing the request. Applies to request-stage workflow changes only.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: When issue, target status, target cycle, or other required workflow-change context is missing or ambiguous, stop and request clarification before preparing the request.

service_topology_bindings.implementation_notes:
Accepted assistant guidance:
- Formalize the capability surface each service must expose: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
  Why: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.
- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
  Why: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.
- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
  Why: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.
- Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
  Why: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative issue and comment scenarios show the service gathers required inputs, returns a preview payload, and clearly indicates that approval is required before creation or posting.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Issue preparation and comment preparation produce usable previews for review while stopping before any unapproved Linear mutation occurs.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Review scenarios show transition and move requests generate approval-ready previews, return clarification when target context is missing, and block restricted state changes or cross-team moves without the required grant.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Status transitions and cycle moves are handled as governed requests that stop for approval, with restricted workflow states and cross-team moves prevented unless explicitly granted.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM review of representative flows shows missing team, issue, status, or target container context returns clarification-required, while private team access, workflow bypass, unapproved mutation, and raw export attempts are explicitly denied or restricted.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can search issue, project, and cycle context; prepare issue creation previews; prepare comment previews; request status transitions; and request cycle or project moves through governed capability flows.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: May initiate preview or request flows but should not directly execute creation, posting, restricted transitions, or governed moves without approval. Missing team, issue, target status, or target container context should trigger clarification rather than silent execution.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Uses the governed Linear fronting service to search issue context and prepare issue, comment, status transition, or cycle move requests without direct raw workspace access.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see actor-visible summaries and bounded search results within team allowlists and result limits. Must not see hidden teams, raw workspace exports, or unrestricted GraphQL data.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can provide team-scoped search inputs and prepare work items tied to their allowed team context. Can request moves or transitions only within the governed scope presented by the service.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Does not gain approval authority merely by being team-scoped. Approval is still required for issue creation, comment posting, status transitions, and cycle or project moves where the brief marks them as gated.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: A requester operating within an allowed Linear team scope for bounded discovery and preparation workflows.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can access issue, project, and cycle summaries only for allowed teams and only within bounded result limits. Private team exfiltration and hidden team access are denied.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review issue creation previews, comment previews, status transition requests, and cycle or project move requests. May approve or deny according to governance rules, especially where the flow explicitly stops for approval.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Holds approval authority for at least some gated mutation flows, but the brief does not specify whether authority differs by action type, restricted workflow state, or cross-team move. Those boundaries should be confirmed before finalizing.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Reviews governed previews and requests before mutation actions proceed in Linear.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect the preview or request details, business reason, and approval record context needed to approve or deny governed actions. Broader data visibility beyond the requested item is not established by the brief.

service_topology_bindings.implementation_notes:
Accepted assistant guidance:
- Formalize the capability surface each service must expose: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
  Why: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.
- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
  Why: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.
- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
  Why: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.
- Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
  Why: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

business_constraints.assistant_drafted_requirements_summary:
Expose a governed Linear capability surface instead of raw workspace tools: The product shall provide an ANIP fronting service for Linear that exposes only organization-approved product-work capabilities for issue discovery, issue preparation, comment preparation, status transition requests, and cycle move requests. The service shall not expose raw Linear workspace tools or arbitrary GraphQL operations to agents.
Support bounded, team-scoped issue context search: The product shall support read-only search of Linear issue context using a required team key and query, with bounded result limits and actor-visible summaries only. Search behavior shall remain constrained to approved team scope and must return clarification_required when required context such as team scope is missing or ambiguous.
Require preview-first behavior for issue and comment creation flows: The product shall prepare issue creation previews and issue comment previews without creating or posting anything directly. Issue preparation shall collect required team, title, and description inputs and may include governed metadata such as project, labels, and other approved optional fields. Comment preparation shall collect issue, purpose, and bounded context and return a preview suitable for review before posting.
Gate all write-adjacent Linear actions behind approval boundaries: The product shall treat issue creation preparation, comment preparation, status transition requests, and cycle move requests as approval-gated flows. For each of these actions, the service shall stop at a preview or request artifact and require explicit approval before any downstream mutation is allowed to proceed.
Govern status transition requests with reason capture and restricted-state controls: The product shall support governed status transition requests for a specified issue and target status, with a required business reason. The service shall return clarification_required when issue or target status context is missing or ambiguous, and it shall enforce explicit grant requirements for restricted workflow states rather than allowing direct workflow bypass.
Govern cycle move requests and restrict cross-team moves: The product shall support governed requests to move an issue into a target cycle using a required issue reference, target cycle, and business reason. The service shall stop for approval before any move occurs and shall require explicit grant for cross-team moves.
Return clarification_required when required operational context is missing: The product shall use explicit clarification semantics when required context is absent or ambiguous. At minimum, missing or unclear team, issue, target status, or target container context shall not be guessed and shall instead produce clarification_required so the caller can supply the missing information.
Prevent hidden-team access, raw export, and unapproved mutation paths: The product shall deny private team exfiltration, hidden team access, raw workspace export, arbitrary backend execution, workflow bypass, and unapproved mutation attempts. Governed provider controls may be supported only when they remain bounded and cannot override team scope, approval policy, or export restrictions.
Apply actor-aware visibility and team allowlist controls across all capabilities: The product shall enforce actor-aware visibility and team allowlists across search, preparation, and request flows. Responses shall expose only the level of detail appropriate for the caller's allowed team scope and governance posture.
Preserve approval records and auditability for governed Linear actions: The product shall maintain approval records and audit evidence for governed actions so reviewers can understand what was requested, why it was requested, what policy boundary applied, and whether the action was allowed, restricted, denied, or stopped for approval.
